extraction.one_line_summary: EVENEPOEL attacked inside final kilometer after ROGLIC refused to share pace work, won by 2 seconds but insufficient bonus to take leader's jersey
tactical_patterns: Soudal Quick-Step pace-making on every climb systematically isolated Emirates leadership, splitting Roglic's protection before final ascent.
Evenepoel demanded pace-sharing from Roglic in final 9km knowing refusal would legitimize his own attack without appearing disrespectful.
Marc Soler attacked without protecting teammate Ayuso, revealing UAE team cohesion fracturing under Quick-Step pressure tactics.

rider_signals.ROGLIC Primoz: negative: refused to share pace work with Evenepoel in final kilometers, revealing either tactical weakness or physical limitation under sustained Quick-Step pressure
rider_signals.EVENEPOEL Remco: positive: converted Quick-Step's early investment into stage win by forcing Roglic to refuse cooperation, then attacking with legitimacy inside 1km
rider_signals.BERNAL Egan: negative: dropped on sustained climbing despite gradual pace, suggesting recovery timeline remains protracted even on sub-threshold efforts
rider_signals.SOLER Marc: negative: attacked without supporting Ayuso's GC position, indicating UAE team hierarchy confusion or individual ambition overriding tactics
rider_signals.CARAPAZ Richard: neutral: refused to quit breakaway despite diminishing chances, held position until 9km but lacked punch when group fractured
